item.title: UBS makes progress in its quest for mediocrity in the US
item.description: Bank is aiming for an 18 per cent pre-tax profit margin in the US wealth business by 2028

item.title: Good vibes are masking a reset in markets
item.description: Stock market returns should not distract investors from the new AI trade taking hold

item.title: It is investors vs gamers as Sony ditches discs
item.description: The move is being presented as zeitgeist rather than greedy rent extraction

item.title: Burnham must learn from Starmer’s failures on UK growth
item.description: There are moments to prioritise other things, but after 18 years of historic economic slowdown this is not one of them

item.title: Taylor Swift and the allure of a big celebrity wedding
item.description: These magnificent displays of vulgarity and vainglory offer a glimpse into the most private of worlds

item.title: The heat of the matter
item.description: Why do we so often choose to deny or ignore problems that are right in front of us?

item.title: How to win at quitting
item.description: Serena Williams’s joyful comeback offered a striking contrast to the gloomy retirement of Ben Stokes

item.title: Won’t somebody not think of the children?
item.description: Family-friendly events have made public culture more accessible — and oddly less mature

item.title: As the tech bubble deflates, turn to old-fashioned valuation rules
item.description: Did SpaceX mark the moment the air started coming out? OFVRs will help you ride out the ensuing volatility

item.title: Jay Rayner reviews Appalachia, London: ‘A refreshed take on the London American restaurant’
item.description: Happy birthday, USA. Your politics might be messy, but your food really can be great

item.title: How Bordeaux learnt to take American wine seriously
item.description: The Mondavi-Mouton partnership, Opus One, began as a transatlantic shock — and transformed the reputation of California wine

item.title: An American birthday worth celebrating
item.description: Though its founding ideals are threatened, the US has the capacity for renewal

item.title: Why US exceptionalism in markets is justified
item.description: An honest conclusion is that America’s centrality rests on a set of mutually reinforcing advantages

item.title: Failings on defence leave Britain cruelly exposed
item.description: A desperate urge to save money has led the UK to ignore the dangerous new realities

item.title: How crowds become stupider
item.description: Prediction markets suffer when we all think the same way

item.title: Laying off cigarettes boosts Big Tobacco’s financial vitals
item.description: Despite the decline in smoker numbers, investors are rethinking the industry’s prospects

item.title: What has America ever done for us?
item.description: Jazz? The moon landings? Gerrymandering? On this 250th anniversary, it seems only right to think about this question

item.title: The June jobs report was a blip — we hope
item.description: Plus, tricks with trimmed mean inflation

item.title: Revolut and Monzo should heed Atom Bank’s not-so-tiny challenges
item.description: As these racy unicorns look to bulk up in lending, they will have to navigate some of the same complexities

item.title: Investing in an age of lawlessness
item.description: Copying the investments made by Trump and other political elites is one potential tactic but it carries risks

item.title: Adidas is outplaying Nike in the global sportswear competition
item.description: The momentum is finally with the German group under the leadership of Bjørn Gulden

item.title: All hail the King of the North! But which north?
item.description: Andy Burnham’s fantasy realm will fall apart if it’s allowed contact with messy geographical reality

item.title: ’Tis the season to discover the UK’s hidden garden gems
item.description: The National Garden Scheme offers the opportunity to peek into some little-known plantsman paradises

item.title: When food and fashion collide
item.description: Both get my juices flowing, but it’s something of an odd combination

item.title: The faltering engine of Germany’s auto industry
item.description: VW is a test of the country’s ability to adapt
